addBox(uint40,bytes16,(string,uint256,bo.details: Add a Box Proxy, each is a minimal clone that shares a logic contract and has minimal state - Caller must page inputs if necessary (eg spenders, tokens) - Conditionally deploys a new instance based on `deployed` (used in a migration) - Deployed boxes do not support logic upgrades. To upgrade a box, deploy a new box (with a new address) - Addresses are predictable unless squatted then unpredictable but progress effectively guaranteed

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected seqNum for determinism; See `CallTracker`

addBoxLogic(uint40,bytes16,uint256,addre.details: Add a new logic contract. New boxes will use this version by default - No remove function as it's unlikely this will be called many times

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`
params.version: Version label for the logic, must be unique. 0 not allowed as a sentinel for 'use latest'

approve(uint40,bytes16,(string,uint8,add.details: Allows spender to call transfer on a token directly - CallResult.rc Return code indicating if call was successful or error context, see `IBox.ApproveRc`

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected seqNum for determinism; See `CallTracker`

params.active: Target active boxes when true, else inactive boxes
params.name: search key

constructor.details: Ensures the logic contract cannot be hijacked before the `initializer` runs - Sets version to `type(uint64).max` + `emit Initialized(version)` to prevent future initialization - `initialize` is where the business logic is initialized on proxies - For more info see comments in 'Initializable.sol'

params.active: Target active boxes when true, else inactive boxes

params.creator: Creator's address for access control during setup
params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers

params.reqId: Request ID, unique amongst requests across all callers
params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

push(string,address,(string,uint8,addres.details: Allows caller to use this contract's access control rather than approval per token - No `_isReqReplay` since this call cannot be executed directly from off-chain

params.boxName: Box to push token from (token source)
params.qty: Quantity to push; =0 to push entire balance
params.ti: Token info for a single transfer

params.seqNumEx: =0 for on-chain caller, else expected sequence number for determinism, etc; See `CallTracker`
